title_original: QT Sense closes â¬4M round to support real-time cell analysis
summary_original: Dutch startup QT Sense has secured €4 million—including a €3 million seed round led by Cottonwood Technology Fund, €0.6 million ONCO-Q grant, and €0.4 million Quantum Forward Challenge prize—to develop Quantum Nuova, a quantum-based platform that enables real-time, single-cell analysis of living cells. Unlike conventional methods, Quantum Nuova uses fluorescent nanodiamond quantum sensors to monitor oxidative stress and metabolic changes in living tissues, providing direct insights into disease and drug response. The funding will advance hardware, analytics, and deployment with partners, including applications in colorectal cancer research. The platform has already been used for studying mechanisms of FDA-approved drugs, aiming to support new diagnostics and therapies by revealing cellular behavior previously inaccessible to researchers.
